pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 9-11: Creates project at $WORKSPACE_DIR/$PROJECT_NAME where WORKSPACE_DIR=/home/ga/eclipse-workspace and PROJECT_NAME=RadiationPlanningCore
setup_task.sh line 54: Creates prefs file at $PROJECT_DIR/.settings/org.eclipse.jdt.core.prefs

Official Eclipse JDT documentation and real project examples show the correct property name for resource leak detection is either 'org.eclipse.jdt.core.compiler.problem.unclosedCloseable' or 'org.eclipse.jdt.core.compiler.problem.potentiallyUnclosedCloseable', not 'resourceLeak'. Real projects (Processing, Checkstyle, analysis-core-plugin) use unclosedCloseable, not resourceLeak. The property 'resourceLeak' does not appear in Eclipse's IProblem constants or official JDT Core options documentation.

Official Eclipse JDT Core Options documentation confirms 'org.eclipse.jdt.core.compiler.problem.potentialNullReference' is a valid compiler option with possible values ERROR, WARNING, INFO, or IGNORE. Verified through help.eclipse.org JDT Core Options page and multiple Eclipse projects confirm this property name and value format are correct.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic Eclipse RadiationPlanningCore Java project at /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/RadiationPlanningCore with configuration file at .settings/org.eclipse.jdt.core.prefs. The DosimetryCalculator.java source file intentionally contains a resource leak (unclosed FileInputStream) and potential null pointer access (unguarded doubleValue() call). The metadata correctly identifies the project location and prefs file path. The potentialNullReference compiler setting is correctly named and can be set to 'error'. However, the metadata claims a compiler setting called 'org.eclipse.jdt.core.compiler.problem.resourceLeak' which does not exist in official Eclipse JDT documentation or real project examples; the correct property names for resource leak detection are 'unclosedCloseable' or 'potentiallyUnclosedCloseable'.
